episode_text:
day=30; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Social with person in Front; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=medium; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is fruit or other cold-served food AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including personal, front-TV, and front-social meals, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user consistently avoids reheating foods intended to be served cold. IF food_condition is a warm savory meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a quiet personal setting, including simple morning handheld savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals show a stable preference for serving standard savory foods warm. IF food_condition is a warm savory meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front, including front-social savory meals with fish bites, potatoes, tartar sauce, and a front-social fruit meal, THEN 2 min for savory foods and no microwave for cold fruit BECAUSE social meals preserve the intended serving temperature of the food rather than imposing a single social-context rule. IF food_condition is a warm savory meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, THEN 2 min in general, but 1 min is also observed for a front-laptop morning savory meal with ranch BECAUSE repeated laptop meals usually favor moderate reheating, while one work meal shows a lighter-heating exception. IF food_condition is a mixed meal including brownies or sauce-relevant items AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front or on the left, THEN evidence favors stronger reheating, with 2 min previously observed for a front-TV mixed meal and 3 min for a left-TV bananas-brownies-chocolate-sauce meal BECAUSE dessert-containing TV meals can elicit a hotter-serving preference than simple cold fruit meals. IF food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory meals suggest a stable preference for warming, but not always maximal reheating. IF food_condition is a simple savory handheld or bite-sized meal without dipping needs AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the right, THEN evidence is mixed between 1 min and 3 min, but favor 3 min for hotter savory meals such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli and 1 min for simpler lighter savory items such as bite-sized sandwiches BECAUSE right-TV meals show that food type within the same media context affects desired serving temperature.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed personal meal, including simple savory handheld foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user does not request view preservation when no external social or media target is present. IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including simple savory meals and pizza-and-broccoli meals, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly prioritized preserving visibility toward the right-side TV. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user consistently prioritized keeping the right side visually open for social engagement. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the left-social meal shows the same side-matched visibility preference for social engagement on the left. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including front-social savory meals and a front-social fruit meal, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-social meals show a stable preference to preserve the front visual field for a social partner regardless of whether the meal is savory or cold fruit. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals consistently show a stable preference to preserve the front visual field while using a front-facing device. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals show a stable preference to protect the right visual field when the attended device is on the right. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected front-occlusion minimization while attending to a front-facing TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce and a bananas-brownies-chocolate-sauce meal, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals now consistently show a stable preference to protect the left visual field when attention is directed there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including sauce-optional meals such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals indicate the user does not request left-side visibility protection in that context, suggesting occlusion sensitivity tracks the salience of the attended target rather than simply its direction.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple cold fruit or dessert-like meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the user does not always prefer maximum speed even for simple foods when no external task is present. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a warm savory meal without sauces or dips, including simple handheld foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN medium BECAUSE personal savory meals reinforce medium as the user's baseline balance of comfort and efficiency. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, fish-bites-with-tartar-sauce meals, simple pizza-and-broccoli meals, and a front-social fruit meal, THEN medium BECAUSE social meals across partner locations and food types consistently support medium as the user's balance of comfort and efficiency. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN fast BECAUSE the user explicitly chose faster robot motion in that simple meal context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed or more complex meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a more moderate speed for a more complex front-TV meal. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN slow BECAUSE the front-TV dinner shows a stronger comfort/caution preference for a hot savory meal in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory or mixed meal, including a left-laptop evening meal with chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup, THEN medium BECAUSE the user consistently chose medium speed in repeated laptop-engaged meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory handheld meal, THEN medium BECAUSE earlier right-TV meals used the baseline speed for straightforward foods. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a hotter savory plated meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN slow BECAUSE the newer right-TV pizza meal indicates the user becomes more cautious for hotter or more substantial savory foods even within the same TV context. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory evidence suggests medium is the more stable choice there. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected a more cautious pace for that left-TV dessert-containing meal, indicating that left-TV meals can shift toward slower motion when the food is messier or more delicate.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF feeding foods that require fork skewering AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is typical skewered solid foods such as meats, fish bites, nuggets, potato pieces, hash browns, broccoli-containing savory meals, bite-sized sandwiches, bite-sized pizza pieces, chicken bites, chicken nuggets, or potato wedges, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option across savory meals. IF feeding foods that require fork skewering AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is soft dessert-like or fruit items where acquisition reliability may matter more than narrow bite shape, including brownies, bananas, strawberries with whipped cream, and mixed fruit meals, THEN evidence is mixed, but favor parallel to major axis as the overall default while noting one perpendicular selection for soft dessert-like or fruit items BECAUSE the user's physical need for smaller bites is stable, though they have shown willingness to trade bite narrowness for acquisition reliability for some softer items.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple fruit or dessert-like meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user wanted explicit bite-acquisition verification even outside social or work settings. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a warm savory meal without sauces or dips, including simple handheld foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN yes BECAUSE personal savory meals reinforce a stable preference for explicit verification rather than skipping confirmation for efficiency. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including skewered solid or savory meals, sauce-accompanied savory meals, fish-bites-with-tartar-sauce meals, simple pizza-and-broccoli meals, and a front-social fruit meal, THEN yes BECAUSE social meals across partner locations and food types indicate a stable preference for extra verification when failed transfers would be more awkward. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid, savory, or mixed dessert-containing meal, THEN yes in general, but no is also observed for a front-laptop morning savory meal with ranch BECAUSE the user often prefers explicit bite-acquisition verification while working, yet one front-laptop meal shows that for a straightforward savory work meal the user may prioritize efficiency instead. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that simpler TV context, consistent with preferring efficiency there. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed, sauce-involving, or hot savory meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for more complex front-TV meals, indicating that food complexity in that context triggers a preference for verification. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce or a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals support a stable preference for confirmation in that more complex, attention-divided context. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, THEN evidence is mixed, but favor yes for more substantial plated savory meals such as pizza with broccoli and no for very simple straightforward savory meals BECAUSE right-TV evidence suggests confirmation use increases with meal complexity even when the setting is otherwise efficiency-oriented.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed personal meal, including simple handheld savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and consistently selected the less intrusive transfer style in personal settings. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including front-social savory meals, a front-social fruit meal, and left-social savory meals, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and has consistently selected the less intrusive transfer style in social settings. IF dining context is any previously observed routine or laptop context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any previously observed food condition, including repeated front-laptop savory meals, repeated right-laptop savory meals, and repeated left-laptop savory meals such as chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user has usually selected the less intrusive transfer style across these contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN the evidence is mixed between inside mouth transfer and outside mouth transfer, but favor outside mouth transfer BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence still leans toward the less intrusive style. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected more direct delivery for that left-TV meal, suggesting that in TV contexts softer or messier foods can shift preference toward direct assistance. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the front-TV dinner shows that for some hotter or more effortful savory meals the user prefers more direct delivery even in a front-facing media context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal or a mixed but less effortful meal, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals used the less intrusive style unless the meal appeared to call for more direct assistance. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, THEN evidence is now mixed, with outside mouth transfer for simpler right-TV meals and inside mouth transfer for a pizza-and-broccoli meal, but favor inside mouth transfer for hotter or more effortful plated savory foods and outside mouth transfer for simpler handheld foods BECAUSE right-TV evidence suggests food effortfulness can override the user's usual preference for less intrusive delivery.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth stopping distance does not apply when the robot delivers directly into the mouth. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a quiet personal setting AND food_condition is a simple fruit or dessert-like meal, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stopping distance in the personal setting. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a quiet personal setting AND food_condition is a warm savory meal without sauces or dips, including simple handheld foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN near BECAUSE personal savory meals reinforce a stable preference for a close, easy-to-reach transfer point in personal contexts. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a routine meal AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stopping distance in the routine setting. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, fish-bites-with-tartar-sauce meals, simple pizza-and-broccoli meals, and a front-social fruit meal, THEN medium BECAUSE social meals across partner locations and food types show the user prefers a slightly larger interpersonal buffer in social settings. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a left-laptop evening meal with chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup, THEN near BECAUSE the user repeatedly returned to the closer stopping distance in laptop-engaged contexts. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals used the closer stopping distance when outside-mouth transfer was selected. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN near BECAUSE newer left-TV evidence indicates the user can prefer a close stopping distance there even when attention is divided. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the right AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected the close stopping distance in right-TV meals whenever outside-mouth transfer was used.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed personal meal, including simple handheld savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal meals show a preference for salient multimodal readiness cues when no social or media distraction makes speech undesirable. IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken readiness cues in the routine setting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, fish-bites-with-tartar-sauce meals, simple pizza-and-broccoli meals, and a front-social fruit meal, THEN LED BECAUSE social meals across partner locations and food types support a stable preference for quieter visual cues. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a left-laptop evening meal with chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a visual cue while engaged with a device. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a visual cue while attending to TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce and a bananas-brownies-chocolate-sauce meal, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated left-TV evidence consistently supports a quieter visual-only cue in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, THEN evidence is mixed between LED and speech + LED, but favor speech + LED for more substantial or hotter savory meals such as pizza with broccoli and LED for simpler right-TV meals BECAUSE right-TV evidence suggests the user wants more salient prompting when the meal demands more careful coordination.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including personal meals, repeated laptop meals, side-social meals, front-social savory meals, front-social fruit meals, and TV meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, can easily press buttons, and limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less suitable; the user has consistently selected explicit manual signaling across contexts.

IF dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including personal meals, repeated laptop meals, side-social meals, front-social savory meals, front-social fruit meals, and TV meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and has consistently preferred explicit manual readiness signaling for drinking across contexts.

IF dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including personal meals, repeated laptop meals, side-social meals, front-social savory meals, front-social fruit meals, and TV meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and has consistently preferred explicit manual readiness signaling for mouth wiping across contexts.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed personal meal, including simple handheld savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal meals show a preference for salient multimodal completion cues when no social or media distraction makes speech undesirable. IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken completion cues in the routine setting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, fish-bites-with-tartar-sauce meals, simple pizza-and-broccoli meals, and a front-social fruit meal, THEN LED BECAUSE social meals across partner locations and food types support a stable preference for visual completion cues. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a left-laptop evening meal with chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a visual completion cue while engaged with a device. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a visual completion cue while watching TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce and a bananas-brownies-chocolate-sauce meal, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated left-TV evidence consistently supports a quieter visual-only completion cue in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, THEN evidence is mixed between LED and speech + LED, but favor speech + LED for more substantial or hotter savory meals such as pizza with broccoli and LED for simpler right-TV meals BECAUSE right-TV evidence suggests the user wants more salient completion prompting when the transfer itself is more demanding.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including personal meals, repeated laptop meals, social meals on the left, right, and front, front-social fruit meals, and TV meals, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable automatic completion method for bites across contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including personal meals, repeated laptop meals, social meals on the left, right, and front, front-social fruit meals, and TV meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling for drinking rather than automatic completion across contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including personal meals, repeated laptop meals, social meals on the left, right, and front, front-social fruit meals, and TV meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling for mouth wiping rather than automatic completion across contexts.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed personal meal, including simple handheld savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN no BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user does not request retraction when no external visual target needs protection. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, fish-bites-with-tartar-sauce meals, simple pizza-and-broccoli meals, and a front-social fruit meal, THEN yes BECAUSE social meals across partner locations and food types show the user consistently chooses retraction when social engagement and visibility matter. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals show the user accepts longer meal time to reduce obstruction while maintaining visibility of a front-facing task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals consistently show the user accepts longer meal time to keep the attended right visual field clear. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose retraction when front visibility mattered. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce and a bananas-brownies-chocolate-sauce meal, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show a stable preference to clear the view when a left-side attention target is present. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal, including both straightforward handheld foods and pizza-and-broccoli meals, THEN yes BECAUSE accumulated right-TV evidence now favors preserving visibility to the attended TV even when that increases meal time. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including a left-laptop evening meal with chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup, THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-laptop evidence indicates the user does not want extra retraction time in that context despite working, making left-laptop an exception to the user's usual work-related visibility preference.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is a meal without sauces or dips AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including personal savory meals, personal bite-sized sandwiches, front-social fruit meals, front-social pizza-and-broccoli, and right-TV savory meals including pizza-and-broccoli, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping when dipping was unnecessary. IF food_condition includes a light dessert-like topping or dip AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a quiet personal setting, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN less BECAUSE the user accepted some topping application in the personal setting but still preferred restraint rather than heavy dipping. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front, including front-social fish-bites-with-tartar-sauce meals, THEN less BECAUSE social meals across partner locations support a restrained dipping preference consistent with reducing mess during social engagement. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front with a simple fruit meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid dipping despite sauce being available, consistent with an efficiency or low-mess preference in that meal. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, THEN less in general, but do not dip is also observed for a front-laptop morning savory meal with ranch BECAUSE the user often chooses restrained dipping while multitasking, and work contexts usually favor limited rather than heavy sauce use, yet one front-laptop meal shows that in some work contexts the user prefers to avoid optional dipping entirely to minimize mess and interaction overhead. IF food_condition includes a savory meal where sauce is part of the dish rather than a separate dip to apply AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop in front, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user distinguished between optional dipping and already-sauced foods. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front with a mixed meal including dessert or sauce-relevant items, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected limited dipping rather than none, indicating a preference for modest flavor addition without heavy mess. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left, including savory meals with sauce and a bananas-brownies-chocolate-sauce meal, THEN less BECAUSE repeated left-TV sauce-involving meals reinforce a stable preference for restrained sauce use in that attention-divided context.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple fruit or dessert-like meal, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected an intermediate timeout, suggesting a balance between efficiency and protection against unwanted automatic progression in personal settings. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a warm savory meal without sauces or dips, including simple handheld foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE personal savory meals reinforce the same intermediate timeout as the user's personal baseline. IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user preferred a short timeout, consistent with efficient progression when explicit button controls are available. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, left, or front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, fish-bites-with-tartar-sauce meals, simple pizza-and-broccoli meals, and a front-social fruit meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE social meals across partner locations and food types show a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when attention may be shared. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a left-laptop evening meal with chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup, THEN 1000 sec in general, but 10 sec is also observed for a front-laptop morning savory meal with ranch BECAUSE the user usually chooses a very long timeout while working to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided, yet one front-laptop meal shows that in at least some straightforward work meals the user is comfortable with a short timeout and more efficient flow. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short timeout in that simpler TV context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a more complex mixed or sauce-involving meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long timeout there, indicating that in TV contexts food complexity can shift the user toward stronger protection against unwanted automatic continuation. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the front-TV dinner shows an intermediate timeout preference, suggesting the user may want some protection against unwanted continuation without the extreme delay used for more complex or multitasking contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN the evidence is mixed between 10 sec and 1000 sec, but favor 1000 sec BECAUSE earlier left-TV savory evidence leaned toward strong protection against unintended automatic progression in that divided-attention context. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, including bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a short timeout in that left-TV meal, indicating that left-TV timeout preference also depends on food type and can shift toward efficiency for softer dessert-like foods. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory handheld meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE earlier right-TV evidence favored strong protection against unintended automatic progression even for simple meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a more substantial savory plated meal such as pizza with broccoli, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the newer right-TV pizza meal indicates the user may accept a moderate timeout when the meal is still attention-dividing but the overall interaction pattern is more deliberate and cue-rich.
